name: @liteship/core
description: The heart of LiteShip: define UI boundaries, tokens, themes, and signals once as a content-addressed graph, then drive the engine that keeps every rendered output in sync.
author: Eassa Ayoub <eassa@heyoub.dev>
//exports-allowlist: SECURITY + LAYOUT-LOCK: the `exports` map is a CLOSED allowlist — only `.`, the nine curated DOMAIN facades (`./authoring`, `./reactive`, `./motion`, `./graph`, `./evidence`, `./schema`, `./media`, `./clock`, `./wasm`), `./testing`, `./harness`, `./simulation`, and `./fs-walk` are importable. Each domain subpath resolves to a `src/<domain>/index.ts` facade that is EXPLICIT curated named re-exports only (no `export *`), so the subpath surface is the reviewed public surface of that domain — never the raw internal layout. There is DELIBERATELY no `"./*"` wildcard and NO `./internal` subpath: a wildcard would expose every `src/*.ts` module as a public subpath, leaking the internal layout and preventing safe refactoring, and `./internal` holds the un-exported leaf utilities (rng/fnv/numeric/type-level/…). The retained `"./validated-output": null` deny is belt-and-suspenders: validated-output.ts (now under `src/evidence/`) holds `mintValidated`, the sole mint site for the un-forgeable apply token, and exposing it as a public subpath would let a consumer forge a ValidatedProposal and bypass validation (lesson #12 — the TIER_TARGET_SETS subpath leak). Internal relative imports `./evidence/validated-output.js` are unaffected. Both halves (no-wildcard + the deny) plus the closed domain allowlist are guarded by tests/unit/meta/validated-output-subpath-leak.test.ts.
//peer-fast-check: OPTIONAL peer: the core surface (`.`) needs none of it, but the opt-in `./harness` subpath (arbitrary-from-schema) imports fast-check at RUNTIME to realize schema-derived arbitraries. Declaring it here (optional) closes the public-subpath dependency closure — a fresh consumer importing `@liteship/core/harness` is told to provide fast-check, instead of silently relying on a hoisted dev copy (the Wave-8 fast-check scar). Guarded by tests/unit/devops/declared-dependency-closure.test.ts.
